title: -Loan Modifications for Existing Housing Trust Fund Projects: Margaret Breland Homes (2577 San Pablo Avenue) and Mable Howard Apartments (3250 Sacramento Street)

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution approving amendments to existing Housing Trust Fund (HTF) loan
documents for Margaret Breland Homes and Mable Howard Apartments to:
1. Approve the transfer of ownership of both properties to Berkeley Senior Recap,
L.P., a new ownership entity formed to apply for Low-Income Housing Tax
Credits (LIHTC), with Resources for Community Development (RCD) as the
Managing General Partner;
2. Approve the addition of the future tax credit investor limited partner at the time of
the construction loan closing, as required by the LIHTC program;
3. Approve the extension of the City’s current loans and regulatory agreements for
both properties to new 55-year periods and the reduction of interest rates from
6% simple interest to the Applicable Federal Rate (AFR) in effect at the time of
construction loan closing (currently approximately 4.8%); and
4. Authorize the City Manager or their designee to execute all documents required
to effectuate these actions, including amendments to existing loan agreements.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
There are no new City funds requested for these actions. The proposed loan
modifications extend existing City loans and facilitate the leveraging of LIHTC equity
and tax-exempt bond financing to address significant capital needs. The City’s HTF
investments in the projects—$2,719,108 for Margaret Breland Homes and $982,479 for
Mable Howard Apartments—will remain secured by the existing deeds of trust, and
affordability requirements will be maintained through the regulatory agreements. The
modifications will strengthen the long-term financial viability of both projects and
preserve 68 deeply affordable senior housing units in Berkeley.

Loan Modifications to Margaret Breland Homes CONSENT CALENDAR

sections.current_situation:
RCD is seeking City approval for the loan modifications as part of a strategy to complete
a HUD Rental Assistance Demonstration (RAD) conversion and apply for LIHTC and
tax-exempt bond financing for the rehabilitation of both properties. Both properties are
20–25 years old and have accumulated capital needs that exceed available reserves.
The proposed RAD conversion will allow RCD to convert existing HUD 202 rental
assistance contracts into a single 20-year Housing Assistance Payment (HAP) contract
for both properties, increasing operating revenue and enabling the projects to support
new additional debt for much-needed rehabilitation.
RCD intends to apply for LIHTCs in Round 1 of 2026 and anticipates a combined $13
million rehabilitation, or approximately $190,000 per unit in hard construction costs.
Planned improvements include modernization of building systems, energy and
accessibility upgrades, and enhanced safety and functionality.
On October 9, 2025, the Housing Advisory Commission (HAC) approved the loan
modification request for Margaret Breland Homes and Mable Howard Apartments with
the following vote:
Action: M/S/C (Simon-Weisberg/Twu) to recommend City Council to:
1. Approve the transfer of ownership of both properties to Berkeley Senior Recap,
L.P., a new ownership entity formed to apply for Low-Income Housing Tax
Credits (LIHTC), with Resources for Community Development (RCD) as the
Managing General Partner;
2. Approve the addition of the future tax credit investor limited partner at the time of
the construction loan closing, as required by the LIHTC program;
3. Approve the extension of the City’s current loan terms for both properties to new
55-year periods and the reduction of interest rates from 6% simple interest to the
Applicable Federal Rate (AFR) in effect at the time of construction loan closing
(currently approximately 4.8%); and
4. Authorize the City Manager or their designee to execute all documents required

sections.background:
Both properties are long-term HTF-supported senior affordable housing developments:
 Margaret Breland Homes (2577 San Pablo Avenue) – 28 units, completed in
2006, City loan of $2,719,108; all units affordable to households ≤50% AMI, with
most tenants at ≤30% AMI.
Page 2
Page 3 of 5
Loan Modifications to Margaret Breland Homes CONSENT CALENDAR
and Mable Howard Apartments December 2, 2025
 Mable Howard Apartments (3250 Sacramento Street) – 40 units, completed in
2000, City loan of $982,479; all units affordable to households ≤50% AMI, with
most tenants at ≤30% AMI.
Each property is currently owned by an RCD-controlled nonprofit entity and managed by
the John Stewart Company. The RAD conversion and new HAP contract will stabilize
operating revenues while maintaining affordability for all residents. Tenants will continue
to pay no more than 30% of their income toward rent. The proposed loan
modifications—extension to 55 years and adjustment of the interest rate to the AFR—
will allow RCD to maximize equity investment and ensure the long-term preservation of
these properties.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The rehabilitation will improve building systems, reduce energy and water use, and
modernize mechanical and electrical components, thereby reducing greenhouse gas
emissions and supporting the City’s sustainability goals.
sections.rationale:
The proposed modifications are necessary to enable RCD to secure tax credit and bond
financing, complete rehabilitation, and preserve 68 affordable senior units for the long
term. The City’s loan extensions and interest rate adjustments maintain regulatory
protection while enabling RCD to leverage substantial outside investment with no new
City funds.
sections.alternatives:
Without the proposed modifications, the projects would be unable to pursue LIHTC
financing or complete the RAD conversion, placing their long-term affordability and
physical condition at risk.
CONTACT PERSON
Lourdes Chang, Senior Community Development Project Coordinator, HHCS
(510) 981-5263

Internal
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
APPROVING LOAN MODIFICATIONS FOR MARGARET BRELAND HOMES (2577
SAN PABLO AVENUE) AND MABLE HOWARD APARTMENTS (3250 SACRAMENTO
STREET)
WHEREAS, the City Council established a Housing Trust Fund Program (HTF) to guide
the City’s work to assist in the development and expansion of housing affordable to low-
and moderate-income people who either work or reside within the City of Berkeley (City),
and authorized the City Manager to implement the Program; and
WHEREAS, the City provided Resources for Community Development (RCD) with HTF
loans of $2,719,108 to Margaret Breland Homes and $982,479 to Mable Howard
Apartments to support the development and long-term affordability of these senior
housing projects; and
WHEREAS, both projects are owned and operated by an RCD-controlled nonprofit entity,
providing a total of 68 affordable senior housing units; and
WHEREAS, both properties have operated successfully for over 20 years but now require
substantial rehabilitation to replace aging systems, improve accessibility, and ensure
continued habitability and safety; and
WHEREAS, RCD proposes to complete a HUD Rental Assistance Demonstration (RAD)
conversion and apply for Low-Income Housing Tax Credit (LIHTC) and tax-exempt bond
financing to fund the rehabilitation of both properties, without requesting new HTF
funding; and
WHEREAS, to complete this financing, RCD requests that the City approve: (1) the
transfer of ownership of both properties to Berkeley Senior Recap, L.P.; (2) the addition
of the future LIHTC investor limited partner; (3) the extension of the existing City loans
and regulatory agreements to new 55-year terms; and (4) the modification of the interest
rate from 6% simple interest to the Applicable Federal Rate (AFR) at the time of
construction closing; and
WHEREAS, the City finds that these loan modifications will preserve long-term
affordability, ensure the rehabilitation of 68 affordable senior un
